title: Enchanted Moroccan Cheese Tagine
description: A tangy, shaggy Moroccan tagine that blends aromatic spices with creamy colby jack cheese, finished with a crisp yakimono grill sear for a romantic dining experience.
instructions: Heat a large heavy‑bottomed pot over medium heat and add the olive oil.
Sauté the diced onion until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, and tomato paste, and stir to coat the onion mixture.
Pour in the homemade vegetable broth, bring to a gentle simmer, and let it reduce slightly, about 5 minutes.
Toss in the cubed sweet potato and chopped apricots, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes until the sweet potato is tender.
Reduce the heat to low, stir in the shredded colby jack cheese, and let it melt, about 5 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, stirring gently until everything is integrated.
Transfer the tagine to a yakimono grill or a preheated grill pan set to medium‑low and sear the top until a golden crust forms, 3-4 minutes.
Remove from heat, let rest for 2 minutes, then serve hot over a bed of fresh herbs or a small side of cauliflower rice.
